title: Is the zero-divisor graph Γ(Z_73 × Z_25) prime?
generalization_signal: Verified exact feeder for the live Γ(Z_p × Z_25) complementary-support slice: at p = 73 the fixed-A0 / high-barrier mechanism still works, and the prime-only C pool being short by one is repaired cleanly by the single complementary-support label 25.
candidate_theorem_slice: A fixed-A0 / high-barrier / complementary-support slice for Γ(Z_p × Z_25): if A = A0, B = {1,b1,b2,b3} with distinct barrier primes above half the interval, and C has size p - 1 with every prime divisor avoiding {2,3,b1,b2,b3}, then the four-class reduction closes and Γ(Z_p × Z_25) is prime.
next_action: Run generalize on the verified z73 feeder inside the Γ(Z_p × Z_25) campaign, then test z79-z25-prime-zero-divisor-graph as the next late-regime discriminator before any instance-specific Lean work.
strongest_honest_claim: An explicit prime-labeling witness for Γ(Z_73 × Z_25) survives skeptical verification: the classwise partition A = A0, B = {1,193,197,199}, C = all allowed primes up to 384 plus 25, and D the residual complement gives a bijective labeling with coprime labels on every actual edge. This is still an unformalized feeder result, but it is strong evidence for the broader complementary-support C slice rather than for the obsolete strict prime-only corollary.
